headline: Momentum stocks hit 4th worst stretch in 22 years
detail: The Mag-7 led selloff is crushing the exact momentum/growth names your book is built on. Historically this kind of momentum washout resolves higher 70% of the time, but the short-term pain is real — AVGO, PLTR, and CRWD all sit in this blast radius. On margin, you don't always get to wait for the recovery.

headline: BIS warns AI frenzy could trigger market slump
detail: The Bank for International Settlements flagging AI-driven market risk is a direct overhang on your chip and AI names — AVGO, SMH, CRWD, PLTR all carry heavy AI premium in their valuations. If institutional money starts repricing AI risk, your most concentrated names get hit hardest. This adds to the macro case for trimming your SELL-flagged positions.

headline: China economy picks up on rebounding U.S. exports
detail: A stabilizing Chinese economy is a modest positive for semiconductor demand — AVGO and SMH both have meaningful China revenue exposure. This is a rare green flag in an otherwise rough tape for your book, but it's a slow-moving tailwind against a fast-moving selloff.

headline: Grantham shifting cash into high-flying tech
detail: A noted bear moving into tech names is a sentiment data point worth noting — it suggests some smart money sees value emerging after the pullback. Not a reason to add today, but relevant context if you're deciding whether to hold SMH (your only green position) through the noise.
